title: Hypotheses, Riskiest Assumptions, and Disposable Prototypes · Mastery Review
summary: A review board compares near-miss decisions for hypotheses, riskiest assumptions, and disposable prototypes and accepts only observable behavior, human evidence, explicit non-claims, and owned transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for ranked assumption map and prototype evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Express desirable, usable, feasible, viable, ethical, accessible, secure, and operable assumptions as falsifiable claims.
Prioritize assumptions by uncertainty, consequence, dependency, and cheapest discriminating evidence.
Choose paper, clickable, coded, data, integration, or operational prototype fidelity to answer one question without production overinvestment.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Prototype Disposal
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A polished coded prototype appears persuasive, but it tests no predeclared assumption and quietly becomes production code after one positive comment. The quiz variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Separate disposable exploration from production code and record what can and cannot transfer after the experiment.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Prototype Disposal.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate disposable exploration from production code and record what can and cannot transfer after the experiment. For Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Prototype Disposal, keep Prototype Disposal connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A polished coded prototype appears persuasive, but it tests no predeclared assumption and quietly becomes production code after one positive comment. The quiz variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Separate disposable exploration from production code and record what can and cannot transfer after the experiment.

content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the Prototype Disposal symptom in ranked assumption map and prototype evidence during this quiz case.

The quiz variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. For Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Prototype Disposal, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A prototype that appears to work should become the production foundation.

stimulus.title: Prototype Disposal failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A prototype that appears to work should become the production foundation.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate disposable exploration from production code and record what can and cannot transfer after the experiment.

options.text: For diagnose the hidden failure, apply Prototype Disposal without its stated preconditions, omit the ranked assumption map and prototype evidence trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Prototype Disposal for ranked assumption map and prototype evidence.
Run the Prototype Disposal boundary case for ranked assumption map and prototype evidence, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

Accept the Prototype Disposal result because visible ranked assumption map and prototype evidence output is the highest authority. That claim fails the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary for Prototype Disposal.

Order the Assumption Register evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Prototype Disposal
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Prototype Disposal: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A polished coded prototype appears persuasive, but it tests no predeclared assumption and quietly becomes production code after one positive comment. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate disposable exploration from production code and record what can and cannot transfer after the experiment.
